designDNA.concept: Royal Gujarati Palace — a festive, luxurious wedding identity rooted in Gujarati cultural ceremony (mehendi, garba, baraat, pheras) expressed through a modern Western lens. Deep jewel-tone backgrounds meet gold marigold accents, glassmorphic surfaces, and architectural palace motifs to create a celebration that feels both ancient and contemporary.
designDNA.tone: Festive, warm, majestic, celebratory, and spiritually grounded — radiating joy and royal grandeur without losing intimacy
designDNA.community: Modern Western with Gujarati Hindu cultural heritage — English-language, diaspora-aware, blending Indian ceremonial aesthetics with clean contemporary layout principles
designDNA.styleDirection: Royal Palace Glassmorphism — deep jewel-tone gradients as backdrop, frosted glass cards and surfaces, gold marigold ornamentation, Mughal-inspired architectural silhouettes, mandala geometry, and diya flame accents layered over a luxurious dark base

shapeLanguage.borderRadius: 999px for pills and eyebrows; 24px for detail cards
shapeLanguage.sectionRadius: 44px top, 130px bottom for palace-arch silhouettes; 50% elliptical for floor glows

visualMotifs: diya oil lamps with flame glow
circular date seal with radial gold gradient
designDNA.textureStyle: Deep jewel-tone radial gradient base (indigo → wine → ruby) overlaid with a fine 72px CSS grid mesh masked to a radial fade, ambient elliptical saffron glow at footer, and soft circular halo blurs at corners — creating a velvety, candlelit depth without photographic textures
designDNA.shadowStyle: Layered deep shadows: large-radius box-shadows (60–110px) at low opacity (0.18–0.36) for floating depth; warm golden glow shadows on flame and mandala elements; inset highlights (1px rgba white) on glass surfaces; no harsh drop shadows

preferredAnimations: gentle glow pulse on diya flames
soft fade-up entrance for copy blocks

designDNA.layoutPersonality: Asymmetric two-column editorial grid (copy left, illustrated visual right) collapsing to single-column centered on mobile; generous clamp-based fluid spacing; max-width 1320px container; visual hierarchy: oversized serif names → italic ampersand → body prose → glassmorphic detail cards
sectionRules: Maintain the deep jewel-tone backdrop (indigo–wine–ruby gradient) across all sections as the unifying canvas
Always use Playfair Display or Cormorant Garamond for headings and large display text; Manrope for all body and UI text
Preserve the gold-marigold (#f7c35f / #d8a84f) as the primary accent color for dividers, icons, labels, and highlights
Use glassmorphic frosted surfaces (rgba cream at 8–15% opacity + backdrop-filter blur 14–20px + 1px rgba cream border) for all cards, modals, and panels
Incorporate at least one Gujarati cultural motif per section (toran, mandala, diya, arch, rangoli, or floral pattern) as a decorative accent
Keep all section backgrounds dark — never introduce a fully white or light background; use cream overlays at very low opacity only
Use pill-shaped (999px radius) badges and eyebrow labels consistently throughout
Maintain the saffron-to-ruby warm glow at section footers/transitions as a visual throughline
Cormorant Garamond italic should be used for the ampersand, dates, and romantic accent phrases
Detail cards and information blocks must use the 3-column or stacked glassmorphic grid pattern established in the hero
Avoid flat, minimal, or Scandinavian-white aesthetics — every section must feel warm, layered, and festive
Avoid stock photography or realistic imagery — all visuals should be CSS-illustrated or SVG-based to match the hero's illustrated palace style
Do not introduce any cool blue, teal, or neon color families — the palette must remain within the warm saffron–ruby–indigo jewel range
Spacing rhythm must use clamp() fluid values to preserve the hero's responsive feel across all breakpoints
Preserve the radial ambient glow treatment (elliptical gradient at section bottoms) as a section-transition device

cssVariables.--grid-mesh: linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(255,244,220,0.06) 1px, transparent 1px), linear-gradient(0deg, rgba(255,244,220,0.045) 1px, transparent 1px)
